data.title: EHang Holdings: Banking On Scalable UAM Model For Growth
data.description:
Introduction & Investment Thesis

I am reiterating my previous bullish rating on EHang Holdings Limited (NASDAQ:EH) driven by its integrated UAM platform. In my last article, I focused on EHang’s eVTOL business model and highlighted the certification successes it has attained from thousands of flight tests to becoming China’s first eVTOL company to receive passenger-carrying certification. Still, EH216-S continues to be EHang’s main revenue driver with its commercial operations in Q2'2025, recording 700 safe flights, delivering 68 units and receiving 150 new orders, which demonstrates strong demand. This highlights the long-term potential of my previous article, and I believe there is yet more upside potential. Having said that, the fact that its core business, which is eVTOL, continues to soar does not negate the fact that there are opportunities in its diversified portfolio. The company has a vision to build smart cities, provide aerial tourism, logistics services, and emergency medical transport services.

For example, let’s look at the vision of building a smart city. The Smart City Expo & Tomorrow Mobility World Congress 2024, held in Barcelona, is an eye-opener for companies pioneering smart cities eVTOL utilities, starting with EH216-S. From a broader perspective, the global eVTOL market is projected to reach $1.5 trillion by 2040, driven by a steady increase in demand for urban applications, including cargo delivery, passenger transport, and aerial imaging, among others. Smart cities will be a major driver of this growth.

From my previous article, you now understand that EHang is in the final stages of mass production for commercialization after the type certificate. From this angle, I am finding out what opportunities EHang sees in smart cities. Given the growth projection of smart cities, which is at a CAGR of 15.5% between 2024 and 2030, it is right to say that EHang is eyeing to seize this opportunity.

Based on my perspective above, I believe the company is creating a wide revenue base from smart cities, which leads me to my explanations below on how I think EHang is positioning itself for the smart cities’ applications.

Smart Cities Are Key To EHang's Growth

I will begin exploring what EHang is looking forward to in its vision of having an integrated vertical UAM platform. In my view, the smart cities market opportunity is the major driver in this diversification. For example, the smart cities market volume is estimated to reach $1.610 trillion by 2030 from $588.4 billion in 2023. This is expected to grow at a CAGR of 15.5% between 2024 and 2025.

One of the major drivers of smart cities is the growing concern for sustainability, particularly in how cities can reduce their environmental footprint. For instance, half of the global population resides in cities and is responsible for 70% of the global greenhouse gases. Furthermore, investors continue to show interest in smart cities, with current investment of around $1 billion. This indicates there is a commitment to the sector’s innovation and growth. So far, there are over 1,003 smart city startups, which means that the road to autonomous mobility is also inevitable. From a global perspective, South Korea and Singapore lead in the implementation of smart cities; however, the United States is expected to generate the most revenues from the smart city market in 2025, at approximately $27.06 billion. This hints at the current state of the smart city market and the future growth opportunity.

EHang is currently entering into partnerships to facilitate market entry into emerging smart cities, such as EHang’s debut at the Smart City Expo World Congress in Spain. This expo included cooperation with Vertiports Network, a European Advanced Air Mobility (AAM). Since the expo included governments, enterprises, and research and innovation firms, it is right to say that EHang is at the forefront of planning urban transportation and smart cities.

More cooperation has been made, including EHang’s partnership with Reignwood Aviation to deploy EH215-S. This partnership is facilitating low-altitude tourism, but the two companies plan to expand the service to aerial logistics, passenger transportation and emergency response. Additionally, EHang has an agreement with the Anshun Government, which has resulted in a purchase order of 50 units of EH216-S autonomous eVTOLs. EHang has also entered into a RMB 1 billion partnership with the Hefei Government for the establishment of the new VT35 series, which is also autonomous, long-range, and offering lift and cruise services.

Upon examining these agreements, it becomes clear that EHang is preparing to lead the smart low-altitude city’s low-altitude mobility and other applications. For instance, when I examine Abu Dhabi’s smart city plan, the city is ambitious to transform its urban mobility into smart urban mobility, utilizing eVTOL air taxis and other services such as transportation. In these plans, EHang is involved in Abu Dhabi’s smart urban mobility. EHang has confirmed that it is planning to establish its regional presence in this city, including a final assembly line and other support services. So far, EHang has conducted a flagship of its autonomous eVTOL EH216-S. This flagship, a collaboration between EHang and Multi Level Group (MLG), introduces a new sky taxi mode and other means of cargo transportation through low-altitude transportation, typically renowned as the next-generation transportation.

Considering smart city growth as the major driver to an integrated UAM platform, partnerships are the first step EHang is taking as an entry mechanism using its EH216-S series. The EH216-S series is pilotless, and this autonomy means that it is a smart eVTOL ready for smart cities. Therefore, instead of using EH216-S in logistics, EH17-L is using EH217-L for the logistics ecosystem, which has a maximum payload of 250 kg. This eVTOL utilizes cloud computing and autonomy, with no need for manual intervention.

In my view, EHang is gradually preparing to enter the smart city ecosystem through partnerships that broaden its revenue base from passenger eVTOLs to an integrated UAM platform offering logistics, smart city management and aerial media. As the first eVTOL to receive complete suite certifications, I believe EHang will capture a bigger share of smart city urban mobility revenue from the overall smart city revenue of $1.380 trillion by 2030.

EH Stock - Financials And Valuation

The company is performing well in terms of revenue with Q2'2025 recording 44.2% YoY to RMB147.2 million ($20.5 million). This represented a significant improvement from its previous quarter’s revenue of RMB 26.1 million ($3.6 million), reflecting an upside of 464%. This was primarily driven by the unit’s delivery, which increased from 11 units in the first quarter of 2025 to 68 units of the EH216 series in Q2’2025. From an annual perspective, its revenue has been on solid upward momentum over the last couple of years, turning a -68.46% YoY growth in 2021 into a consistent revenue improvement in the subsequent years culminating in a 288.46% YoY growth in 2024. Although the growth could normalize due to the normal growth curve, I expect it to remain strong backed by the reasons I discussed above.

You may wonder why I am bullish, despite the company having reduced its revenue guidance from RMB900 to RMB500. I do not see this compressed guidance as a reason to worry because, from my analysis, the company is building partnerships to scale the demand for its eVTOLs. This aligns well with the company’s rationale, which is to establish a solid foundation for its products as the first mover to capture a larger market share in terms of product manufacturer and as a service provider. The company is still conducting flight tests in Hefei, and this gives me a reason to understand why, despite entering into these partnerships, it first needs to perform flight tests before mass production and commercialization. I am maintaining a long-term outlook because the company is focusing on building a broad revenue base from its integrated UAM platform rather than chasing short-term hype. The compressed guidance is an intentional decision for EHang as it seeks to prioritize modernizing its 2025 delivery to more scalable, safe and regular commercial eVTOL operations.

Nevertheless, the company still maintains a strong gross margin of 62.5%, a slight improvement from its first quarter of 2025, at 62.4% which means it is retaining a larger portion of its revenues after paying its operating costs. This is a sign of operational discipline and efficiency.

In terms of profitability, EHang remains a loss-making entity, with Q2 2025 recording a net loss of RMB 81.0 million ($11.3 million), a 13.1% increase from RMB 78.4 million ($10.8 million). This increase in net loss was driven by a non-operating expense of RMB13.7 million ($1.9 million) for the 2023 US securities class action. This is a short-term hiccup that does not impact the long-term development, and I angle and reiterate my bullish outlook. Overall, in terms of annual profitability, the net loss is declining steadily as the company approaches mass production and initiates partnerships, except for the TTM loss, which has gone up as explained previously.

Most importantly, considering the company's trailing total operating expenses of $78.9 million and its current cash and short-term investments of $156.32 million, it has a cash runway of 1.98, which is approximately 2 years. I consider this a strong liquidity position as it shows the sustainability of the company since it's a loss-making firm.

Again, looking at the trailing revenue growth of about 87% and the last two years' revenue growth in the north of 100%, I am seeing normalizing revenue growth and as such, I will assume an average annual revenue growth rate of 60% between 2025 and 2027 to estimate the company's financial performance. Given these assumptions, I project a revenue of about $256 million by 2027, which is in close proximity to consensus estimates of $236 million in 2027.

Looking at profitability, the company has made significant strides with its net margin improving from about -750% in 2023 to its current level of -54.45% which marks an improvement of more than 92% hinting at profitability soon.

While I don't expect this high growth rate to be sustained, I will assume a growth rate of about 20% in 2026 and 2027 because the curve seems to be flattening which is normal towards profitability as opposed to when losses were high and costs were spread on a small revenue base. Given this assumption, I estimate a net income margin of 10.89% in 2026 and 13.07% in 2027, implying that EH is likely to break even next year.

Moving to valuation, I will compare EHang’s valuation against that of its competitors, Cadre Holdings, Inc. (CDRE) and V2X, Inc. (VVX), using the P/S metric. The EHang P/S metric suggests that it is trading at a premium given its P/S of 17.37x compared to its peers with CDRE at a P/S ratio of 2.20x and VVX at a P/S ratio of 0.43x.

This may suggest that EHang is overvalued. However, I think that this is justified given its high trailing revenue growth YoY at 84.04% which is significantly high compared to its competitors, whose revenue growth YoY ranges below 8%. Given the company catalysts I have discussed above which allude to a bright revenue outlook and owing to its trailing growth relative to its peers, I think its premium valuation is justified.

Besides its growth which warrants its premium pricing, I also find the company better positioned than its peers due to several reasons. I give two examples. First off, EH is a pure play autonomous aerial vehicle company as opposed to CDRE and VVX, who are traditional defense and tactical solutions thus lacking the exposure to the fast-growing UAM at a CAGR of 35% between 2024 and 2030.

Secondly, EH has a leaner operational structure with less than 500(483) employees as opposed to VVX's more than 16,000 and CDRE's more than 2,000 staff respectively. This lean setup grants them lower operational costs which leads to strong gross profit relative to its peers. This in my view has the potential for shorter innovation cycles both from the cost perspective and with fewer complexities in larger operational setups.

To me this is a major advantage in emerging tech sectors as it is likely to lead to new innovations with ease thus the possibility of staying ahead of the curve. In my view, this also explains its first-mover advantage in the industry.

Investment risks

Legal exposure – the company is facing a U.S. Securities Class Action case filed in 2023, which is draining its cash reserves through settlement fees used for the same purpose.

Regulatory risks – The company is taking the time to conduct flight tests with its partners before proceeding to full-scale production. This is delaying its revenues.

Conclusion

I am reiterating my previous bullish rating in this article. I believe that the integrated UAM platform positions EHang for increased revenue generation as it plans to capture a larger share by facilitating smart city next-generation transportation.

data.title: AbbVie Smashes Earnings, Bets Big On Depression Drug Bretisilocin
data.description:
Since my last article, "Why AbbVie's Stock Is A Strong Buy After Q1 2025 Results," AbbVie's stock price (NYSE:ABBV) (NEOE:ABBV:CA) has risen 11.5%.

It currently trades near its 52-week high of $209.5, thanks in part to strong sales of Skyrizi and Rinvoq, which were key contributors to beating Wall Street's consensus revenue estimate by $392.7 million.

In this article, I will provide an analysis of AbbVie's acquisition of Gilgamesh Pharmaceuticals' bretisilocin and its financial results for Q2 2025.

AbbVie made a smart move by acquiring Gilgamesh's bretisilocin

Before I discuss the deal itself, I want to go back a bit and remind you how the relationship between these two companies began.

On May 13, 2024, AbbVie entered into a partnership agreement with Gilgamesh Pharmaceuticals worth up to $2.06 billion to develop next-generation neuroplastogens to develop next-generation neuroplastogens to treat people with a variety of mental disorders.

And now, just over a year later, on August 25, 2025, AbbVie announced the acquisition of Gilgamesh's bretisilocin for up to $1.2 billion.

You, like me, may have a few questions.

Why does AbbVie need bretisilocin?

Overall, AbbVie has a strong position in the CNS therapeutics market thanks to Vraylar, Botox, Ubrelvy, Vyalev, and Qulipta. And its former CEO Richard Gonzalez and current CEO Robert Michael are working to reduce AbbVie's financial dependence on sales of its immunology franchise.

However, the failure of its two Phase 2 EMPOWER studies evaluating emraclidine for schizophrenia, the success of this strategy was somewhat shaky, and, in my view, what was needed was a product candidate that combined a promising mechanism of action and already demonstrated extremely encouraging efficacy in the treatment of a widespread disease.

And as a result, this experimental drug became bretisilocin.

What is the mechanism of action of bretisilocin?

It is a 5-HT2A receptor agonist and 5-HT releaser.

In simple terms, bretisilocin activates a receptor called 5-HT2A, which leads to an acceleration of the formation of connections between neurons and thus helps a patient with depression to reduce the number of negative thoughts circulating in his head and also to look at his problems/experiences from a different angle.

Plus, unlike such widely used antidepressants as Eli Lilly's Prozac (LLY) and Pfizer's Zoloft (PFE), the mechanism of action of which is aimed at inhibiting the reuptake of the well-known hormone serotonin [5-HT], bretisilocin, on the contrary, causes its release, thereby acting faster in transmitting signals directly related to motivation or a sense of satisfaction with life.

Thus, I make the first conclusion, and it is that, only taking into account the mechanism of action of bretisilocin, it represents a promising product candidate for the treatment of moderate-to-severe MDD.

Were the results of the Phase 2a clinical trial strong?

On May 27, Gilgamesh Pharmaceuticals published the results of a Phase 2a study involving 40 people suffering from moderate-to-severe major depressive disorder.

According to the data, taking bretisilocin led to a statistically significant decrease in the MADRS [Montgomery-Åsberg Depression Rating Scale] compared to those in the low-dose psychoactive comparator group.

In addition, it demonstrated a faster antidepressant effect in the first 24 hours, and in my opinion, what is more important and clearly shows the efficacy of bretisilocin [GM-2505, 10 mg] is that 70% of patients achieved MADRS remission by day 14 from the start of treatment compared to 25% of those in the comparison group.

Also, Gilgamesh stated in its press release that the safety profile of bretisilocin was promising, with no serious adverse events.

Overall, I analyzed the clinical data of Axsome's Auvelity (AXSM) and Johnson & Johnson's Caplyta (JNJ), and I can say that AbbVie was not mistaken in purchasing bretisilocin in my view, which demonstrated higher efficacy in the fight against this common mood disorder, which, according to NIH, affects 21 million Americans.

AbbVie's revenue continues to grow amid strong sales from Skyrizi and Rinvoq

So, AbbVie's revenue was $15.42 billion in the second quarter of 2025, beating the consensus estimate by $392.7 million.

It increased 6.6% year-over-year and 15.6% quarter-over-quarter, which to me is a clear indication that declining Humira sales [$1.18 billion in Q2 2025 vs. $2.81 billion in Q2 2024] and the weak performance of its Eye Care franchise are no longer having a significant impact on AbbVie's total revenue.

Its non-GAAP EPS also did not disappoint, reaching $2.97, up 20.7% quarter-over-quarter, and beating my expectations by 5 cents.

At the same time, AbbVie's growing R&D expenses due to the acquisitions of Capstan Therapeutics, Nimble Therapeutics, and Aliada Therapeutics, as well as the concluded collaboration agreements that I discussed in the article "Why AbbVie Remains A Strong Buy For Long-Term Investors," did not have the negative impact I had previously factored into my model.

So why did its revenue rise so sharply?

In the table below, I have marked the sales of AbbVie's key medications.

What can be understood from this table? What conclusions do I draw?

First, competition from medications like Johnson & Johnson's Stelara, Eli Lilly's Taltz, Bristol Myers Squibb's Sotyktu (BMY), and Novartis' Cosentyx (NVS) also can't slow down the growth of AbbVie's immunology franchise revenue, which amounted to about $7.63 billion in Q2 2025, up 21.8% quarter-on-quarter.

Sales of its superstar Skyrizi [risankizumab] reached $4.42 billion, beating my expectations by $220 million and also up 62.2% from Q2 2024 due to increased demand for it to treat people with psoriatic arthritis and ulcerative colitis.

Meanwhile, sales of AbbVie's second and equally important medication, Rinvoq [upadacitinib], were about $2.03 billion in the second quarter of 2025, up 41.8% year-on-year and 18% quarter-on-quarter.

Now it's time to discuss the performance of its oncology franchise.

So, its net revenues were $1.68 billion for the three months ended June 30, 2025, thus continuing to tread water in the last 4 quarters, which, in my opinion, is not surprising.

Why?

I believe the dark spot of this franchise is Imbruvica [ibrutinib], whose sales reached $754 million, down 9.5% from Q2 2024.

It is, as I mentioned in the article "Top 4 Immunology Stocks Poised For Growth In 2025", an inhibitor of Bruton's tyrosine kinase and has lower efficacy than AstraZeneca's Calquence (AZN) and BeOne Medicines' Brukinsa (ONC).

On the other hand, Elahere [mirvetuximab soravtansine] sales were $159 million in Q2 2025, beating my expectations by $14 million thanks to its accelerated adoption into clinical practice for the treatment of certain patients with ovarian cancer.

How did AbbVie's margins change?

Its gross profit and gross profit margin were about $11.08 billion and 71.8% for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up $817 million and 0.9%, respectively, year-on-year.

AbbVie's operating income margin was 37.5%, up 6.2% quarter-on-quarter, driven by strong performance from its immunology franchise, which I discussed in detail earlier, as well as increased sales of Ubrelvy and Qulipta, approved for the treatment of people with migraine.

At the same time, I would like to point out that it exceeds the operating income margins of its key competitors in the anti-inflammatory and CNS therapeutics markets, such as Johnson & Johnson [28.9%], Pfizer [28.7%], and Sanofi [18%] (SNY).

Has AbbVie made progress in reducing its total debt/EBITDA ratio?

I believe the importance of this question remains high as AbbVie remains active in the M&A space, particularly in pursuit of targets that allow it to strengthen its position in the cancer or mental health markets.

Overall, I wrote in detail about the partnerships and acquisitions that AbbVie has made since 2019 in the article "Why AbbVie Remains A Strong Buy For Long-Term Investors".

AbbVie's total debt was about $70.6 billion at the end of June 2025, down $353 million year-on-year at a rate of 22.3%.

Yes, this number may scare you a little, but I believe it is more important to look at its total debt/EBITDA ratio.

It was 2.51x and continues to move towards the 2x mark, mainly due to AbbVie's growing EBITDA [$7.84 billion in Q2 2025 vs. $7.13 billion in Q2 2024]. At the same time, its credit ratings from S&P Global Ratings [A-, stable outlook] (SPGI) and Moody's Investors Service [A3] (MCO) are at relatively high investment grades.

Did AbbVie use its share repurchase program in Q2?

Unlike the first quarter, when AbbVie bought back its shares for about $961 million, in Q2 2025, its CEO, Robert Michael, decided not to use its share repurchase program.

In my opinion, this decision is quite justified given the acquisition of Nimble Therapeutics and Capstan Therapeutics [up to $2.1 billion in cash], as well as the fact that its short interest remains at an extremely low level according to data provided by Fintel.

Risks

I have updated the table with risks to include my concerns about declining sales of Imbruvica, Duodopa, Alphagan/Combigan, and Trump's ongoing threats and loud statements against Big Pharma.

Takeaway

So, in conclusion, I want to mention again that AbbVie continues to do what it does best, which is succeed in commercializing its blockbusters, including Skyrizi, Rinvoq, Ubrelvy, Epkinly, and Vyalev.

In my opinion, their strong performance, especially over the last year and a half, has allowed AbbVie to remain active in the M&A space with the ultimate goals of reducing its free cash flow [FCF] dependence on sales of the immunology franchise and strengthening its position in the mood disorder treatment market.

In addition, in part because it raised its full-year 2025 adjusted diluted EPS guidance from $11.67-$11.87 to $11.88-$12.08, implying 18.4% year-over-year growth, I continue to cover AbbVie with a 'Strong Buy' rating.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

So that's super important as far as being able to set ratings alerts for your entire portfolio really quickly.

This quiz goes out every Friday and we'll send a link in the chat to the page that it goes out and you can follow that page and you'll get an alert whenever the quiz is published so that you'll be able to see it. And like I said, it goes out every Friday. Just a fun way to test your knowledge on kind of the news of the week, to make sure that you're staying up-to-date and see how you stack up against others.
